comparisons.ground_truth_name: Free Cash Flow Margin for latest fiscal year
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Free Cash Flow Margin: Represents the ratio of free cash flow to revenue for the full year 2024, using fiscal year-end data or the last set of audited annual results.

comparisons.kpi_name: AI building- build tailored, practical AI models
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback: evidence overlap with Quality Criteria (5=High).
comparisons.ground_truth_name: AI ethics governance model yes or no
comparisons.ground_truth_answer: Yes — Orange S.A. has a formal AI ethics governance model, including a Group Data & AI Ethics Charter (published 2022) overseen by an independent Data & AI Ethics Council reporting to the Executive Committee, and dedicated bodies such as a Responsible AI Design Authority (risk classification for each AI project) and a centralized Responsible AI Committee for complex decisions, with “Responsible AI by design” and human oversight embedded across the AI lifecycle.
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Evidence shows Orange has: (1) an independent Data & AI Ethics Council reporting to the Executive Committee and a Data & AI Ethics Charter published in 2022 and deployed group-wide [4][20]; (2) a Responsible AI Design Authority to qualify risk for each AI project and a centralized Responsible AI Committee for decision‑making on complex AI governance questions [2]; (3) a “Responsible AI by design” approach with human oversight built into development and operations, aligned to EU AI Act governance [1][3]. Collectively, these confirm AI ethics embedded in its governance model.

comparisons.kpi_name: Business deployment- integration into on-going operations
comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback: evidence overlap with Quality Criteria (5=High).
comparisons.ground_truth_answer: Orange S.A. has formal AI ethics documentation: its Data & AI Ethics Charter (published in Nov 2022) is deployed group-wide and sets an overarching framework for ethical, responsible and transparent use of data and AI. This is operationalized through a “Responsible AI by Design” governance approach and overseen by dedicated bodies—the Data & AI Ethics Council, a Responsible AI Design Authority, and a centralized Responsible AI Committee—that cover risk classification, human oversight, bias mitigation, and alignment with the EU AI Act.
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: Orange explicitly states it created and published a Data & AI Ethics Charter in November 2022, to be deployed across the Group [9][21][18]. It also details a “Responsible AI by Design” philosophy and formal governance with a Responsible AI Design Authority and a centralized Responsible AI Committee to classify risks and ensure ethical use, alongside the Data & AI Ethics Council [2]. Orange further notes alignment of its practices with the EU AI Act and its own Charter [16][2].

comparisons.pipeline_rationale: Heuristic fallback: evidence overlap with Quality Criteria (5=High).
comparisons.ground_truth_answer: Orange S.A. operates a formal AI governance framework comprising: (1) an independent Data & AI Ethics Council reporting to the Executive Committee that defines the Group’s ethical framework for data/AI, oversees use cases, and monitors deployment of the Data & AI Ethical Charter; (2) a Responsible AI Design Authority that classifies the risk level of each AI project and a centralized Responsible AI Committee that decides on complex deployment, accountability and risk issues; (3) a secure internal AI platform providing centralized governance, integrated GDPR safeguards and end‑to‑end lifecycle support under a “Responsible AI by Design” approach with embedded human oversight and intervention mechanisms; and (4) enterprise risk and board‑level oversight via the Group Risks Committee and the Board’s Audit Committee (risk management and controls), Strategy & Technology Committee (technology strategy) and Governance & Corporate Social and Environmental Responsibility Committee (charters and compliance programs). The company also aligns with the EU AI Act through risk assessment/mitigation and regular audits and participates in the EU AI Pact.
comparisons.ground_truth_explanation: This summary consolidates Orange’s cited AI oversight bodies and mechanisms: the Data & AI Ethics Council (independent, accountable to the Executive Committee, publishes and oversees the Data & AI Ethical Charter) [1][12]; the Responsible AI Design Authority and a centralized Responsible AI Committee for AI risk qualification and deployment/accountability decisions [2]; a secure internal AI platform with centralized governance and GDPR safeguards, plus a “Responsible AI by Design” approach with human oversight across the lifecycle [2]; risk oversight via the Risks Committee and board committees—Audit (risk management/control effectiveness), Strategy & Technology (strategic/technological orientations), and Governance & CSER (charters and compliance programs) [3][5]. Proactive EU AI Act alignment, including risk management planning and regular audits, and participation in the EU AI Pact are also stated by Orange [2].
